description:
Compiler-confirmed dead code. Remove everything the compiler warns about.

FIELDS of CompiledModule — never read:
  module: JITModule       (populated but never accessed)
  schemas: Vec<Schema>    (populated but never accessed)
  types: ModuleTypes      (pub(crate), never accessed)

METHODS of CompiledModule — never used:
  static_closure_targets(&self)
  schema_for(&self, fn_id) -> &Schema
  run(&self, fn_id) -> i64
  run_in(&self, fn_id, process) -> i64
  run_internal(&self, fn_id) -> i64  (private)

FREE FUNCTION — dead outside test cfg:
  join_return_descrs (line ~775): used only in test code; move inside #[cfg(test)]
  or delete if the tests that call it are also being cleaned up.

DUPLICATE ATTRIBUTE — double #[cfg(test)] at lines ~6089-6090: remove one.

For each deletion: if removing a field requires removing its population site in
compile_with_backend or compile(), do so. If a method's removal creates a type error
(e.g., a trait impl references it), remove the impl too.

After removal, cargo build must produce zero warnings and all tests must pass.
title: fz-cg3.3 dead code removal: CompiledModule fields, methods, join_return_descrs, double cfg
